title: Phase 3: Settings UI
description:
## Scope

Phase 3 delivers a full-page settings view at #/settings with tabbed Global/Local column
editor, discovery settings, drag-reorder, validation, save/reset, and dirty detection.

## Deliverables

- [ ] #/settings route with gear icon navigation in header
- [ ] createSettingsView() factory following view factory pattern
- [ ] Tabbed layout (Global / Local) with ARIA roles
- [ ] Column CRUD: sortable list, add/edit form, delete, drag-reorder, keyboard reorder
- [ ] Conditional params UI (status for status-issues, issue ID for issue-detail)
- [ ] Auto-suggest drop_status based on subscription type
- [ ] Discovery settings editor (scan_roots list, scan_depth number, global only)
- [ ] Local tab: inherit/override radio toggle with column editor in override mode
- [ ] Dirty detection via JSON.stringify comparison
- [ ] Save via save-settings WS message, reset to server state
- [ ] External change handling: silent update when clean, toast when dirty
- [ ] Full CSS using BEM naming and CSS variables
- [ ] Tests for lifecycle, CRUD, validation, dirty state

## Dependencies

- Depends on: Phase 0 (save-settings protocol, per-project infrastructure)
- Required by: nothing

title: Phase 1: Responsive Kanban Columns
description:
## Scope

card condensation at narrow widths, and ResizeObserver-driven layout updates. Eliminates
horizontal scroll for the default 5-column layout on 1920px displays.

- [ ] `computeColMinWidth()` utility function in `app/views/board.js`
- [ ] CSS grid with `minmax(var(--board-col-min-width), 1fr)` replacing fixed `1fr`

- [ ] `.board-card--condensed` CSS class (< 260px: hide type badge, reduce padding)
- [ ] `.board-card--minimal` CSS class (< 180px: title only)
- [ ] ResizeObserver with 100ms debounced recalculation
- [ ] Post-render card class toggling based on column width
- [ ] Unit tests for `computeColMinWidth` and integration tests for resize behavior

## Dependencies

- Depends on: nothing (parallel with Phase 0)
- Required by: nothing (Phase 2 integrates with responsive grid via --board-columns)

title: Phase 3: Kanban Filters
description:
## Scope

Phase 3 delivers client-side filtering for the dynamic Kanban board: a filter bar with three dropdown selectors (Parent/Epic, Assignee, Type) above the board grid, with AND logic, localStorage persistence, and automatic dropdown population from issue data across all column stores.

## Deliverables

- [ ] Filter state in AppState with localStorage persistence
- [ ] Dropdown population logic extracting unique values from stores
- [ ] Filter bar component with three select dropdowns
- [ ] Client-side filtering in refreshFromStores() with AND logic
- [ ] Filter bar CSS styling
- [ ] Unit tests for filter rendering and logic

## Dependencies

- Depends on: Phase 1 (dynamic columns must exist before filtering across them)
- Required by: (none - final phase)
design:
## Architecture

Filter bar component added to board.js template() rendering above the board-root div. Client-side filtering applied in refreshFromStores() using Array.filter() with AND logic on column_data Map values. Filter state persisted in localStorage following existing beads-ui.board pattern. Dropdown population scans all column_data Map entries for unique values.

## Key Dynamic Column Types

- col_defs: ColumnDef[] array defining columns ({id, label, subscription, params?, drop_status})
- column_data: Map<string, IssueLite[]> keyed by column ID (display data after filtering)
- column_raw: Map<string, IssueLite[]> keyed by column ID (raw data before closed filter)
- IssueLite: typedef in board.js (needs parent and assignee fields added for filtering)

## Patterns

- Filter select: see app/views/board.js columnTemplate() closed filter select (lines 162-184)
- State management: see app/state.js AppState typedef and createStore()
- localStorage: see app/main.js existing beads-ui.board persistence pattern (lines 417-432)
- Store composition: see app/views/board.js refreshFromStores() (line 638)

title: Discovery settings, Local tab, save/reset
description:
## Files

- `app/views/settings.js` (modify)

## Implementation

- [ ] Discovery settings section in Global tab (below column editor):
  - Section header: "Discovery (global only)"
  - Scan roots: render list of current roots with [x] remove button per root
  - Add root: text input + "Add" button, appends to draft_discovery.scan_roots
  - Scan depth: `<input type="number" min="1" value="${draft_discovery.scan_depth}">`
  - Store as `draft_discovery: { scan_roots: string[], scan_depth: number }` in closure state
  - Discovery section hidden when Local tab is active (global-only per PRD)
- [ ] Local tab implementation:
  - Radio toggle: `<label><input type="radio" name="local-mode" value="inherit" checked=${!local_override}> Inherit global columns</label>`
  - Radio toggle: `<label><input type="radio" name="local-mode" value="override" checked=${local_override}> Override with custom columns</label>`
  - When "Inherit": show read-only list of global columns (no edit/delete/reorder)
  - When "Override": show full column editor (reuse columnListTemplate and columnFormTemplate) populated from `draft_project_columns` (clone of saved_project.board.columns or empty array)
  - Track `local_override` boolean state, toggle on radio change
- [ ] Dirty detection:
  - `isDirty()` function: compare draft state vs saved state using JSON.stringify
  - For Global tab: compare `draft_columns` vs `saved_global.board.columns` AND `draft_discovery` vs saved discovery
  - For Local tab: compare `local_override` state and `draft_project_columns` vs `saved_project`
  - Show visual indicator (e.g., asterisk in Save button) when dirty
- [ ] Save button:
  - Determine scope from activeTab: 'global' or 'project'
  - Build settings payload based on scope:
    - Global: `{ board: { columns: draft_columns }, discovery: draft_discovery }`
    - Project override: `{ board: { columns: draft_project_columns } }`
    - Project inherit: `{ board: {} }` (empty board section removes override)
  - Send via transport: `await transport.send('save-settings', { scope, settings })`
  - Disable button during request (busy state)
  - On success: update saved state from response, clear dirty flag
  - On error: display error in aria-live region
- [ ] Reset button: deep clone saved state back into draft state, re-render
- [ ] External change handling:
  - Listen for `settings-changed` event via `transport.on('settings-changed', handler)`
  - Store the unsubscribe handle for cleanup in `clear()`
  - If form is clean (!isDirty()): silently update saved and draft state, re-render
  - If form is dirty: show notification "Settings changed externally. Save to keep your changes or Reset to load the new settings."
    - Use `app/utils/toast.js` if it exports a toast function, otherwise render inline notification with `aria-live="polite"`
- [ ] Clean up transport listener in `clear()`

## Pattern

Follow save/reset pattern from `new-issue-dialog.js` (busy state, error handling).
Transport send/on follows `app/ws.js` client API: `send(type, payload)` returns Promise,
`on(type, handler)` returns unsubscribe function.

  - Add `<section id="settings-root" class="route settings" hidden></section>` to the SPA shell template (after board-root, before detail-panel)

  - Import and create settings view: `const settings_view = createSettingsView(settings_root, store, client)`

  - Export `createSettingsView(mount_element, store, transport)` returning `{ load, clear }`
  - `load()`:
    1. Request current settings: `const { settings } = await transport.send('get-settings')`
    2. Request project settings: `const { settings: project_settings } = await transport.send('get-project-settings')`

       - Tab panels: `<div role="tabpanel">` for each tab, shown/hidden based on activeTab
    5. Tab click switches activeTab and re-renders
  - `clear()`: `mount_element.replaceChildren()` and reset state
  - Local state: `{ activeTab: 'global', saved_global, saved_project }`

## Pattern

Follow `createBoardView()` factory pattern in `app/views/board.js` (line 58): factory
function receiving options, returning `{ load, clear }`. Use lit-html `render()` for
templates. Tab pattern uses native ARIA roles per PRD specification.

title: Visibility persistence and reconciliation
description:
## Files

- `app/views/board.js` (modify)

## Implementation

- [ ] On visibility toggle (checkbox change): persist state to localStorage:
  - Key: `beads-ui.board-col-vis:<workspace_path>` where workspace_path comes from `store.getState().workspace.current.path`
  - Value: JSON.stringify of column_visibility object
- [ ] On board view `load()`: read visibility state from localStorage:
  - Parse stored JSON, apply to column_visibility state
  - If no stored state, default all columns visible
  - Handle invalid JSON gracefully (fall back to all visible)
- [ ] Reconciliation function `reconcileVisibility(stored, col_defs)`:
  - For each column in col_defs: if column.id exists in stored, use stored value; otherwise default to visible (true)
  - Remove entries from stored that no longer exist in col_defs (prune stale entries)
  - Return reconciled visibility object
- [ ] Call reconciliation on load() after reading from localStorage
- [ ] Call reconciliation when settings-changed event fires and column definitions change:
  - The board view is rebuilt on settings change (existing behavior in main.js)
  - On rebuild, load() runs again, which reads localStorage and reconciles
  - Alternatively, if a soft settings update occurs without rebuild, reconcile inline
- [ ] Persist the reconciled state back to localStorage (so pruned entries are cleaned up)

## Pattern

title: ResizeObserver and card condensation logic
description:
## Files

- `app/views/board.js` (modify)

## Implementation

- [ ] In the board template function: set `--board-col-min-width` CSS variable on `.board-root` element alongside existing `--board-columns` inline style
- [ ] Create `setupResizeObserver(mount_element)` function:
  - Register `ResizeObserver` on the mount element (board root)
  - On resize callback: debounce to 100ms using setTimeout/clearTimeout pattern
  - In debounced callback:
    1. Get board root dimensions via `getBoundingClientRect()`
    2. Count visible columns from `--board-columns` or col_defs length
    3. Call `computeColMinWidth(rootWidth, colCount, 16, 12)` (gap=16=space-8, padding=12=space-6)
    4. Set `--board-col-min-width` CSS variable on the board root element
    5. Call `updateCardCondensation()` for class toggling
- [ ] Create `updateCardCondensation()` function:
  - Query all `.board-column` elements via `mount_element.querySelectorAll()`
  - For each column: get width via `getBoundingClientRect().width`
  - For each card in the column:
    - Width < 180px: add `board-card--minimal`, remove `board-card--condensed`
    - Width < 260px: add `board-card--condensed`, remove `board-card--minimal`
    - Width >= 260px: remove both classes
- [ ] Call `setupResizeObserver()` at end of `load()` function
- [ ] Disconnect ResizeObserver in `clear()` function:
  - Store observer reference in closure scope
  - Call `observer.disconnect()` in clear() alongside existing cleanup (line ~1017)
- [ ] Call `updateCardCondensation()` after each `refreshFromStores()` render cycle to handle new cards

## Pattern

Follow existing cleanup pattern in `clear()` (line ~1017): unsubscribe listeners, reset state,
replaceChildren. The ResizeObserver is a modern API - no polyfill needed (Node >= 22 implies
modern browser targets). Debounce pattern: store timeout ID, clearTimeout on each call,
setTimeout with 100ms delay.

title: computeColMinWidth utility with tests
description:
## Files

- `app/views/board.js` (modify)
- `app/views/board.responsive.test.js` (create)

## Implementation

- [ ] Add `computeColMinWidth(viewportWidth, columnCount, gapPx, paddingPx)` pure function in board.js:
  - Calculate available width: `viewportWidth - (columnCount - 1) * gapPx - 2 * paddingPx`
  - Divide by columnCount to get per-column width
  - Floor at 180px minimum: `Math.max(180, Math.floor(available / columnCount))`
  - Handle edge cases: columnCount <= 0 returns viewportWidth, columnCount === 1 returns full available
- [ ] Export the function for testing (add to module exports or make it a named export)
- [ ] Create `app/views/board.responsive.test.js` with unit tests:
  - 5 columns on 1920px viewport (gap=16, padding=12): expect ~340-360px range
  - 8 columns on 1920px: expect ~210-230px range
  - 10 columns on 1920px: expect 180px floor
  - 12 columns on 1920px: expect 180px floor (cannot go below)
  - 1 column: returns full available width
  - 0 columns: returns viewportWidth (edge case)

## Pattern

Pure function with no side effects. Place near top of board.js with other utility functions.
Gap value comes from CSS `--space-8` (16px). Padding from `--space-6` (12px).
Test file follows `board.closed-filter.test.js` naming convention for aspect-specific tests.

description:

title: Symlink loop protection
description:
## Files

- `server/discovery.js` (modify, lines 71-79)

## Implementation

- [ ] In walkDir() function at line 71, after the fs.readdirSync call with { withFileTypes: true }:
- [ ] Add entry.isSymbolicLink() check before recursing into directories
- [ ] Option A (simple): Skip all symbolic links entirely - add `if (entry.isSymbolicLink()) continue;` before the isDirectory() check
- [ ] Option B (comprehensive): Track visited real paths via a Set. Before recursing, call fs.realpathSync() on the entry path and check if already visited. This allows non-cyclic symlinks while preventing loops
- [ ] Prefer Option A for simplicity unless the codebase has legitimate symlink use cases
- [ ] Add a debug() log when skipping a symlink for observability

## Testing

- npm run tsc
- npm run lint
- Existing discovery tests in server/discovery.test.js must pass

Currently walkDir at line 77 checks entry.isDirectory() but not entry.isSymbolicLink(). isDirectory() returns true for symlinks pointing to directories, enabling infinite recursion on cyclic symlinks.

title: Phase R4: Discovery and Registry Hardening
description:
## Scope

Phase R4 fixes the TDZ risk in server startup ordering, adds symlink loop protection to directory scanning, adds test coverage for registry-watcher.js, fixes path dedup inconsistency, and addresses watcher cleanup in server/index.js, server/discovery.js, server/registry-watcher.js, and server/list-adapters.js.

## Deliverables

- [ ] H3: Server startup ordering fix (server/index.js)
- [ ] Me6: Symlink loop protection (server/discovery.js)
- [ ] Me8+L8: Path dedup consistency (server/index.js, server/registry-watcher.js)
- [ ] L6: Async workspace scanning (server/discovery.js)
- [ ] L7+I9: Watcher lifecycle management (server/index.js)
- [ ] I4+I8: Minor hardening (server/list-adapters.js, server/registry-watcher.js)
- [ ] Me7: Registry watcher unit tests (server/registry-watcher.test.js)

design:
## Architecture

R4 hardens the server-side discovery and registry infrastructure. H3 fixes a structural TDZ risk by reordering startup calls. Me6 prevents infinite recursion on cyclic symlinks during workspace scanning. Me7 creates the missing registry-watcher test file. Other findings improve path handling consistency and watcher lifecycle management.

## Patterns

- Server startup: see server/index.js lines 84-102 (watchDb/attachWsServer ordering)
- Directory walking: see server/discovery.js walkDir() at line 66
- Registry merge: see server/registry-watcher.js getAvailableWorkspaces() at line 116
- Existing tests: see server/discovery.test.js (pattern for new registry-watcher tests)

- [ ] Create applyBoardFilters() function that iterates all entries in column_data Map
- [ ] For each column entry in column_data: apply Array.filter() with AND logic:
  - If filters.parent is non-null: include only issues where issue.parent === filters.parent
  - If filters.assignee is non-null: include only issues where issue.assignee === filters.assignee
  - If filters.type is non-null: include only issues where issue.issue_type === filters.type

- [ ] Apply filtering AFTER sorting and AFTER ready-excludes-in-progress logic but BEFORE doRender()
- [ ] Apply filtering AFTER getFilterOptions() call (so dropdowns show all available values)
- [ ] For closed columns: apply board filters AFTER applyClosedFilter() (the closed date filter runs first, then board filters narrow further)
- [ ] Column counts in the rendered template automatically reflect filtered totals since column_data Map is updated before doRender()
